name: verify-session-id-via-hooks-server
tags: session id, active session, hooks server, 127.0.0.1:8766, tasks create, set_active, verify before guessing, task:61703ef7
body:
When a tool call needs the current session_id (e.g. tasks__create/set_active), verify it against the hooks server rather than guessing from a project jsonl filename.

Why: guessing from the most-recently-touched .jsonl in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/ can silently pick a stale or wrong session if multiple sessions/files exist for the same project.

**Correction (2026-07-05, task:61703ef7):** GET /session takes NO query params — the `?cwd=<path>` filter this memory previously claimed does not exist in hooks/server.py's actual implementation (it just returns {"sessions": [{"session_id", "turns"}]} for every session in the live checkpointer, unfiltered). Passing ?cwd= is silently ignored, not an error, so the mistake wasn't obvious.

How to apply: prefer the new mcp__claude-hooks__hooks__session_id tool — it returns {session_id, turn} for the current session specifically, with a built-in retry that absorbs the checkpoint-write race (no active task required, unlike /session/active). Fall back to curl http://127.0.0.1:8766/session only if that tool is unavailable, and note it returns ALL sessions (no filtering) — you must disambiguate by turn count/recency yourself.
